threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'bagel shop' instead of a 'beigelry' and order coffee without mentioning oat milk. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a bloke singing opera on the N137 last night, no one batted an eyelid. Brixton nights can be surreal, even sober.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a toddler painting a mural on the sidewalk while their dad brewed coffee on a camping stove. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, it's normal to find a spontaneous rave in an abandoned building at 3 AM. The city's unfinished spaces are its life. Totally unique vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know not to bother with BART during rush hour. You can hear the brakes overheating from two blocks away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught Muni at Civic Center yesterday; a dog on the tracks had commuters more riled up than the usual delays. Only in SF do furry passengers steal the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but it's the little corner bakeries at dawn that breathe life into our mornings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map at Diagonal, clearly convinced they won’t get lost. The locals don’t even glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You carry a reusable coffee cup but haven’t figured out the local cafes all serve cold brew – only the hip ones offer a decent hot latte.
Last night on the 59, a guy tried to barter a can of Fanta for a seat. The driver just shook his head, but you could see the humour in everyone’s eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple of kids selling lemonade on Schillerpromenade, I realized this isn't just a place—it's a community.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble into a hidden U-Bahn station, complete with vintage ads and an echo of the past, if you know where to look.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the Civic Center chaos—while newbies just freeze at the BART detour signs.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like the chaos of a Muni delay due to a dog on the tracks while someone tries to sell you a mixtape through the window.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Plaça de la Vila are for show, but they're actually a hit spot for locals to cool down and meet up on hot days.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to try the caña—a local always knows it’s the small glass, not the pint.
